title: Configuration and environment deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Configuration and environment; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Configuration and environment: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? All configuration files belong in version control because configuration is not code and cannot contain sensitive data.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Version safe shared defaults and schemas, keep credentials out of the repository, document precedence, and validate effective configuration without printing secrets.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Version safe shared defaults and schemas, keep credentials out of the repository, document precedence, and validate effective configuration without printing secrets.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Configuration and environment instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Version safe shared defaults and schemas, keep credentials out of the repository, document precedence, and validate effective configuration without printing secrets.
All configuration files belong in version control because configuration is not code and cannot contain sensitive data.

answer.explanation: Version safe shared defaults and schemas, keep credentials out of the repository, document precedence, and validate effective configuration without printing secrets.
